summary: The highest risk for serious neonatal complications from chickenpox occurs when maternal infection happens within 5 days before or 2 days after delivery, which correlates with the latter stages of pregnancy (weeks 28–37). While congenital varicella syndrome primarily affects infants infected earlier (13–20 weeks), the immediate threat to the newborn—the primary concern here—is greatest near childbirth.
